tier_rationale: Highest natural fit for sovereign self-hosted architecture. Will install, extend, and contribute back. Becomes a power-user evangelist who pulls peers in.
tagline: A technical founder running a productized service or early SaaS who refuses to outsource their operating system to a SaaS vendor.

psychographics.self_identity: I would rather build and own than rent and depend.
psychographics.core_belief: Sovereignty over the stack is a long-term competitive advantage.
psychographics.fear: Vendor lock-in, data hostage, AI moat collapse.
psychographics.ambition: A self-hosted, AI-native business that compounds without subscriptions bleeding it dry.
psychographics.status_anxiety: Building 'just another wrapper' instead of something durable.

pain_points.pain: Patchwork of SaaS subscriptions consuming margin
Wants AI ops but doesn't trust closed cloud vendors with data
Building product steals time from running the business
No content / pipeline strategy — relies on luck
Tools don't compose — every integration is glue code

desired_outcomes.outcome: Self-hosted operating system that owns its own data
AI-native workflows that respect privacy and sovereignty
Composable nodes that extend with their own code
Content engine that doesn't need a marketing hire

buying_triggers.trigger: Just had a SaaS price hike or vendor outage
AI vendor changed terms or model availability
Spent a weekend writing glue code between tools
Hit a wall on growth without marketing systems

objections.counter_angle: You can — but the integration debt will eat 6 months. EPOS is the scaffolding so you build the moat, not the plumbing.
EPOS runs on a single machine; one daemon, no ops team needed.
Code is yours, vault is yours, models are pluggable. Constitutional sovereignty.
EPOS is the OS layer that composes them. Bring your own Postgres, Ollama, Groq, browser-use.

communication_preferences.tone: Technical-peer, principled, anti-corporate, dry humor welcome
communication_preferences.length: Long-form when substance is real; short and dense when not
communication_preferences.vocabulary: Sovereign, self-hosted, composable, primitive, durable, moat
communication_preferences.proof_type: Working code + architecture decisions + operator metrics

conversion_path.awareness: Architecture essay or technical manifesto on sovereign business OS
conversion_path.interest: Open-source/self-install path; technical docs; reference deployment
conversion_path.consideration: Discord community + reference architecture + working examples
conversion_path.decision: Sovereign install (self-serve) or 'bring your stack' onboarding session
conversion_path.retention: Module ecosystem, contribution path, peer community
